pairs.question: When did the inaugural FIS Alpine Skiing World Cup season begin?
pairs.answer: The inaugural FIS Alpine Skiing World Cup season began in January 1967.

pairs.question: What were the start and end dates of the 2016-17 FIS World Cup season?
pairs.answer: The 2016-17 FIS Alpine Skiing World Cup season began on 22 October 2016 in Sölden, Austria, and concluded on 19 March 2017 in Aspen, United States.

pairs.question: Why was the 2016-17 season notable for its finals location?
pairs.answer: The 2016-17 season was notable because the finals were held in North America for the first time in two decades, with the last U.S. finale in 1997 at Vail.

pairs.question: What major event interrupted the 2016-17 World Cup tour, and when did it occur?
pairs.answer: The biennial World Championships interrupted the 2016-17 tour in early February in Saint Moritz, Switzerland.

pairs.question: What were some of the key topics Markus Waldner addressed in his pre-season thoughts for the 2016-17 tour?
pairs.answer: Markus Waldner addressed early season scheduling and weather considerations, growing global interest in alpine skiing beyond Europe and Scandinavia, balancing discipline scheduling with marketability, course construction for safety and competitiveness, and a new regulation allowing top qualifiers to pick starting positions between bibs 1 and 19.

pairs.question: Which notable skiers retired at the end of the 2016-17 World Cup season?
pairs.answer: Former overall World Cup champion Tina Maze of Slovenia and former two-time overall World Cup champion Bode Miller of the United States retired at the end of the 2016-17 season.

pairs.question: How many seasons did Tina Maze miss before retiring in 2017?
pairs.answer: Tina Maze missed the entire 2015-16 World Cup season before retiring in January 2017.
